sourceMovie.title: Young & Beautiful
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Young & Beautiful for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. François Ozon keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Lady Bird
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Young & Beautiful clicked for you, Lady Bird (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Dirty Dancing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Young & Beautiful clicked for you, Dirty Dancing (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Young & Beautiful?
faq.answer: Try Lady Bird, Dirty Dancing, Monster—each shares DNA with Young & Beautiful while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Young & Beautiful?
faq.answer: Young & Beautiful (2013) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Young & Beautiful worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
